subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Core Box
- **181 cards** — Full card pool for menu building.
- **22 menu tiles** — Scoring/reminder tiles for selected card types.
- **8 pawns** — Score tracking pawns.
- **1 game board** — Menu display and score track.

#### Card Pool by Family
- **Nigiri** — 12 cards total.
- **Rolls** — 12 each: Maki Roll, Temaki, Uramaki.
- **Appetizers** — 8 each: Tempura, Sashimi, Dumpling, Eel, Tofu, Onigiri, Edamame, Miso Soup.
- **Specials** — 3 each: Chopsticks, Soy Sauce, Tea, Menu, Spoon, Special Order, Takeout Box, Wasabi.
- **Desserts** — 15 each: Pudding, Green Tea Ice Cream, Fruit.

#### Player-Count Reference List (Step 0 Audit)
- **Game size** — 2-8 players.
- **Dealt cards by player count** — 2-3 players: 10; 4-5 players: 9; 6-7 players: 8; 8 players: 7.
- **Desserts added per round (2-5 players)** — Round 1: 5; Round 2: 3; Round 3: 2.
- **Desserts added per round (6-8 players)** — Round 1: 7; Round 2: 5; Round 3: 3.
- **Maki scoring change** — 2-5 players: 6/3; 6-8 players: 6/4/2.
- **Temaki penalty change** — In 2-player, fewest Temaki does not lose points.
- **Custom-menu restrictions** — Menu and Special Order cannot be used in 7-8 players; Spoon and Edamame cannot be used in 2 players.
- **Tile note** — Maki tile must be flipped for 6-8 player scoring.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
1. **Center board** — Place the game board in the middle of the table.
2. **Choose menu** — Pick a preset menu or build a legal custom menu.
3. **Place tiles** — Put matching menu tiles into board slots so all active scoring is visible.
4. **Dessert pile** — Shuffle chosen dessert cards and place them as a separate face-down pile.
5. **Main deck** — Shuffle all other chosen card sets together as one face-down deck.

--- 2-5 Players ---
- **Maki tile side** — Use standard 2-5 player Maki scoring side if Maki is in the menu.

--- 6-8 Players ---
- **Maki tile side** — Flip Maki tile to 6-8 player side if Maki is in the menu.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
- **Color choice** — Each player chooses a color and takes the matching pawn.
- **Score start** — Place each pawn on or near 0 on the score track.
- **Personal tableau** — During play, keep your face-up cards in front of you, grouped by color/type.
- **Dessert storage** — At round end, move your played dessert cards to your personal side area for final scoring.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
- **Game length** — The game lasts 3 rounds.
- **Round prep** — Before each round, add dessert cards to the main deck based on player count and round number.
- **Deal step** — Deal face-down cards to each player based on player count.
- **Secrecy rule** — Players keep their hands hidden.
- **Remainder deck** — Place leftover cards face down beside the board.
- **Start signal** — Begin simultaneous card selection.

#### Dessert Cards Added Before Each Round
--- 2-5 Players ---
- **Round 1** — Add 5 dessert cards.
- **Round 2** — Add 3 dessert cards.
- **Round 3** — Add 2 dessert cards.

--- 6-8 Players ---
- **Round 1** — Add 7 dessert cards.
- **Round 2** — Add 5 dessert cards.
- **Round 3** — Add 3 dessert cards.

#### Cards Dealt to Each Player
--- 2-3 Players ---
- **Hand size** — Deal 10 cards to each player.

--- 4-5 Players ---
- **Hand size** — Deal 9 cards to each player.

--- 6-7 Players ---
- **Hand size** — Deal 8 cards to each player.

--- 8 Players ---
- **Hand size** — Deal 7 cards to each player.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
1. **Choose** — Every player chooses 1 card from hand and places it face down.
2. **Reveal** — All players reveal chosen cards at the same time.
3. **Resolve effects** — Apply immediate effects and any called bonus effects.
4. **Pass hands** — Pass remaining hand face down to the player on your left.
5. **Continue** — Pick up the new hand and repeat until hands are empty.
6. **Round end** — Score non-desserts, set desserts aside, then reset for next round.

- **Timing priority** — If multiple bonus actions trigger in the same turn, resolve by printed order number (lowest to highest).

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
- **Base action** — On each turn, play exactly 1 card from your current hand.

#### Core Special Cards
- **Chopsticks** — On a later turn, play 2 cards from hand, then return Chopsticks to your hand.
- **Spoon** — Call Spoon to request a named card; first player on your left with that card gives it to you and receives Spoon.
- **Menu** — Draw 4 cards, play 1, return the rest to deck, and shuffle.
- **Special Order** — Copy the card Special Order points at when played.
- **Takeout Box** — Flip previously played cards; those flipped cards are now worth 2 each.
- **Wasabi** — Your next Nigiri is worth x3.
- **Miso Soup** — If any other Miso Soup is played in the same turn, Miso Soup is discarded.
- **Uramaki** — Score race points immediately when 10+ icon thresholds are reached.

#### Custom Menu Construction
- **Required base** — Nigiri is always included.
- **Rolls count** — Include exactly 1 rolls type.
- **Appetizers count** — Include exactly 3 appetizer types.
- **Specials count** — Include exactly 2 special types.
- **Dessert count** — Include exactly 1 dessert type.

#### Player-Count Restrictions
--- 2 Players ---
- **Forbidden cards** — Spoon cannot be used.
- **Forbidden cards** — Edamame cannot be used.

--- 3-6 Players ---
- **Spoon/Edamame status** — Spoon and Edamame are allowed (subject to chosen menu).

--- 7-8 Players ---
- **Forbidden cards** — Menu cannot be used.
- **Forbidden cards** — Special Order cannot be used.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Draft Core
- **Simultaneous draft** — Everyone picks at once, then passes left.
- **Shrinking hand pressure** — Each pass reduces options and increases denial value.

#### Menu System
- **Variable setup** — Different legal menus create different scoring environments.
- **Tile guidance** — Menu tiles are the active scoring reference for the current game.

#### Timing System
- **Priority numbers** — Resolve multi-effect turns by the small printed order numbers.
- **Bonus timing** — Chopsticks/Spoon bonus calls happen during reveal timing before picking up next hand.

#### Immediate vs Delayed Scoring
- **Immediate tracks** — Some cards score during the round (for example, Uramaki race milestones).
- **Delayed tracks** — Dessert cards are banked each round and scored only at game end.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
1. **Hands empty** — End the round when all hands are empty.
2. **Move desserts** — Put dessert cards from this round into each player's dessert area.
3. **Score round cards** — Score all non-dessert cards using active menu tile rules.
4. **Advance pawns** — Move score pawns on the board.
5. **Reset deck** — Collect non-dessert cards, reshuffle with next round's dessert additions, and deal new hands.
6. **Final scoring** — After round 3, score desserts and determine winner.

#### Scoring Sources (Menu-Dependent)
- **Nigiri** — Egg/salmon/squid values; Wasabi triples the next Nigiri.
- **Maki** — Majority scoring by maki icons.
- **Temaki** — Most gains points; fewest may lose points.
- **Uramaki** — 10-icon race with immediate placements and tie rules.
- **Appetizers** — Tempura/Sashimi/Dumpling/Eel/Tofu/Onigiri/Edamame/Miso Soup score by their card rules.
- **Special interactions** — Soy Sauce, Tea, Special Order, Takeout Box, Chopsticks, Spoon, Menu, Wasabi affect scoring/value flow by card text.
- **Dessert endgame** — Pudding, Green Tea Ice Cream, or Fruit score at game end if selected in menu.

#### Player-Count Scoring Differences
--- 2-5 Players ---
- **Maki awards** — Most: 6 points; second: 3 points.

--- 6-8 Players ---
- **Maki awards** — Most: 6 points; second: 4 points; third: 2 points.

--- 2 Players ---
- **Temaki exception** — Fewest Temaki does not lose points.

--- 3-8 Players ---
- **Temaki penalty** — Fewest Temaki loses points per tile rule.

- **Win condition** — Highest total points wins.
- **Tiebreaker** — If tied, tied player with most dessert cards wins.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **First 2-3 picks** — Commit to one scoring lane early instead of splitting into many partial lanes.
- **Wasabi timing** — Draft Wasabi early only when you can realistically pair Nigiri within the same round flow.
- **Race call** — Decide immediately whether you are competing in Maki/Uramaki/Temaki or intentionally denying leaders.
- **Dessert pacing** — Take at least one early dessert pick in dessert-swing menus to avoid desperate round-3 catches.
- **Table read** — Track neighbors' visible columns to predict what cards will be removed before hands return to you.

#### Player-Count Focus
--- 2 Players ---
- **Penalty awareness** — Temaki has no fewest penalty, so tempo around Temaki is less punitive.
- **Restriction awareness** — Spoon and Edamame are unavailable, so plan around other interaction tools.

--- 6-8 Players ---
- **Maki spread** — Third place Maki is worth points, so medium Maki totals can still be efficient.
- **Restriction awareness** — Menu and Special Order are unavailable, so copy/filter lines are removed.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Over-diversifying** — Chasing too many tracks usually creates unfinished sets and weak totals.
- **Dead Wasabi** — Taking Wasabi without a likely Nigiri follow-up wastes tempo.
- **Missed count rules** — Forgetting player-count scoring changes causes bad race math.
- **Timing errors** — Ignoring same-turn order numbers leads to illegal or suboptimal bonus resolution.
- **Late dessert panic** — Waiting until round 3 to contest desserts often fails against earlier commitments.

#### Player-Count Traps
--- 2 Players ---
- **Illegal menu picks** — Do not include Spoon or Edamame in custom menus.

--- 7-8 Players ---
- **Illegal menu picks** — Do not include Menu or Special Order in custom menus.

--- 6-8 Players ---
- **Maki misvaluation** — Do not evaluate Maki like 2-5 player scoring; third place matters here.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Points now vs setup later** — Take immediate points or draft setup cards (Wasabi/Chopsticks lines) that need follow-through.
- **Commit vs deny** — Decide whether to win a race category yourself or spend picks denying the leader's threshold.
- **Dessert tax vs tempo** — Decide each round whether one dessert pick now prevents a larger final swing than your best immediate scorer.

#### Player-Count Lens
--- 2 Players ---
- **Interaction lane choice** — With Spoon/Edamame unavailable, prioritize direct set efficiency and timing tools that remain legal.

--- 6-8 Players ---
- **Maki placement choice** — Decide whether to push for first or secure reliable second/third under 6/4/2 scoring.

--- 7-8 Players ---
- **No Menu/Special Order choice** — Build plans without draw-filter or copy mechanics; commit earlier to visible lanes.

metadata.notes: Written from provided Sushi Go Party! rulebook excerpt and card guide/menu text. Endgame lists all VP source categories referenced in provided text; final exact values always follow the active menu tiles in the selected setup.
